title: mums2
text: begin analyze data, process readable data.frame object can view transform. process data need run two functions: import_all_data(), ms2_ms1_compare(). import_all_data() creates object reflecting MS1 data, ms2_ms1_compare() assigns MS2 spectra MS1 data. point, may need filter noise, transform can properly analyze . accommodate issues, two functions: filter_peak_table(), change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute(). filter_peak_table() allows remove low-quality features change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute() allows transform retention time minutes seconds. allows ensure MS1 data retention time matches MS2 data retention time. explain detail function go pipeline.

title: mums2
text: import_all_data() function takes peak_table meta_data tables input converts mpactr object (wrapper mpactr::import_data()). format parameter accepts three different formats: Metaboscape, Progenesis, None. can read formats (mpactr::import_data()). need deeper understanding format peak_table meta_data file , take look mpactr’s getting started page. Meta_data csv provides information samples. order meta_data valid, needs following columns: “Injection”, “Sample_Code”, ” Biological_Group.” “Injection” column name samples, match samples inside peak_table. “Sample_Code” id technical replicates. Finally, “Biological_Group” another id, biological replicate groups (can learn mpactr::import_data()).
code: data <- import_all_data(   peak_table = mums2::mums2_example("230112_botryllus_peaktable.csv"),    meta_data = mums2::mums2_example("meta_data_boryillus.csv"),    format = "Progenesis") #> If peak table has corrupted compound names they will be converted to #>       utf-8 and if there are any commas, they will be converted to periods(.).

title: mums2
text: importing data, can use functions mpactR filter data. four different filters included mpactR: mpactr::filter_mispicked_ions(), mpactr::filter_group(), mpactr::filter_cv(), mpactr::filter_insource_ions() (can find information mpactR’s website). Although data fitlering required, help reduce noise correct peak selection errors, also speed analysis.

what: Convert from rt to “rt in minutes” or “rt in seconds”
title: mums2
text: Sometimes MS2 file report retention time minutes MS1 file use seconds. mismatch cause MS1 data match incorrect peaks MS2 data. change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute() function allows users synthesize data different units. aware function modifies mpactr object place. Therefore, need call function revert units. display vector retention time.

title: mums2
text: Using generated mpactr_object calling import_all_data() function can use .mgf/.mzxml/.mzml file match MS1 MS2 peaks. ms2_ms1_compare() function reads list mgf files matches MS1 spectra based mass-charge ratio retention time tolerance. multiple matches, select MS2 spectra highest intensity. Keep mind MS2 spectra files memory intensive, can anywhere 1 MB 100 GB. Therefore, depending big file , might take moments function complete. ms2_ms1_compare() generates list data two data.frames (“ms1_data”, “peak_data”), list (“peak_data”), character vector (“samples”). ms2_matches - One two data.frames, “ms2_matches”, data.frame contains five columns: “mz”, “rt”, “ms1_compound_id”, “spectra_index”, “ms2_spectrum_id.” “mz” “rt”, represent MS2 mass charge ratio retention time. “ms1_compound_id” represents MS1 compound id imported feature table. “spectra_index” matches MS2 data MS2 spectrum. Finally, “ms2_spectrum_id” similar “ms1_compound_id”, generated name represent MS2 spectra (name combination mz rt). necessary properly compare compounds. Since compounds similiar structures expected similar MS2 patterns, can use algorithmic techniques compute similarity two spectra. allows us generate annotations cluster similar spectra (MS2 matched information) together. ms1_data - “data.frame”, “ms1_data”, data.frame created mpactr object. peak_data - list generate ms2_ms1_compare() named “peak_data.” “peak_data” collection MS2 peak list. peak list collection fragment ions, value represent intensity mass-charge ratio. samples - last slot inside list character vector named “samples.” list groups/samples contained inside peak_table file.

Using compute_molecular_formulas() function, can generate molecular formulas annotate_ms2() allows us annotate data based reference databases. allows us annotate additional data unknown features confirm known features. explain detail functions can used.

title: mums2
text: add annotation information data, mums2 generate de novo molecular formula predictions using fragmentation trees. compute_molecular_formulas() function uses MS2 data generate explained molecular formula return result (information: Fragmentation Trees). explained molecular formula simply means molecular formula explained peaks compound. order create fragmentation tree generate every possible molecular formula parent mass can create (using CHNOPS). call formulas candidate formulas, formulas potential predicted formulas. look every mass intensity pair inside compound compute every molecular formula. create one directional graph based molecular formulas using. molecular formula point another subformula another formula (meaning contains many atoms parent formula). Finally, can compute score one nodes uses methods like Ring Double Bond equivalents, compute molecular formula score, etc. can learn open-sourced software MZMine Sirius generate molecular formula. Due time function take run, going use small testset. possible formula unable generated. case return NA represent unknown molecular formula. Warning messages printed molecular formula generated one possible molecular formula.

code: dist_obj <- bray_w_omus  pcoa <- cmdscale(dist_obj, k = 2, eig = T, add = T) pcoa_points <- as.data.frame(pcoa$points) variance <- round(pcoa$eig*100/sum(pcoa$eig), 1) names(pcoa_points) <- paste0("PCOA", seq(1:2)) pcoa_points$Samples <- rownames(pcoa_points)  meta_data <- get_meta_data(filtered_data) for(i in 1:nrow(pcoa_points)) {   idx <- which(pcoa_points$Samples[[i]] == meta_data$Injection)   pcoa_points$Samples[[i]] <- meta_data$Biological_Group[[idx]] }  pcoa_points |>   ggplot(aes(x=PCOA1, y = PCOA2, color = Samples)) +     geom_point(size = 5.5) +     theme(       legend.text = element_text(size = 10),        legend.title = element_text(size = 15)     )
